coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Easy shakeout, 4–5 miles at Z2 (130–145 bpm). Your resting HR is 71—elevated 9 bpm above your 30-day average—and recovery score sits at 54/100 (moderate). This signals residual fatigue; you do not need a hard effort today. Friday in taper is a prep day for tomorrow's long run, not a stimulus day. Keep it conversational, relaxed, and use it to check your fueling and pacing feel for the Alps. You're 70 days out; sharpness comes from consistency and freshness, not Friday volume.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: Eat a normal breakfast 2–3 hours before the run (50g carbs, 15–20g protein, low fiber). During the 4–5 mile easy run, you don't need gels or calories—just water. Post-run within 30 minutes: 30–40g carbs + 15–20g protein (chocolate milk, banana + Greek yogurt, or rice cakes with almond butter). Tonight, prioritize carb-loading lightly: aim for 6–7g carbs per kg bodyweight tomorrow in prep for your long run. You're in taper; fuel to support recovery and glycogen, not restrict.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Last night's 8.91 hours at 91.4% efficiency is excellent and masks your elevated resting HR—travel and work stress are likely the culprits, not poor sleep. Protect tonight: aim for 8–9 hours again, dark room, no screens 30 minutes before bed. Your HRV data is absent; if you have a wearable, start logging it now to establish baseline variability before race week. The resting HR elevation tells you to dial back intensity today and tomorrow's pacing should reflect that. Ice bath or contrast shower post-run if you have travel fatigue—20 minutes total.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: You're in week 24 of a 22-week plan (2 weeks over), which means you've earned this taper. The Julian Alps 50K demands alpine legs, vert fitness, and mental toughness on sparse aid—all of which you've built. Friday doubt is normal in taper; resist the urge to "test" yourself with a hard effort. Your job now is trust the training, sleep hard, eat well, and keep the nervous system calm. 70 days is enough time to sharpen for a specific target. Stay disciplined.
